key_points: Forced triples and quads in converted study lounges are real
Rhoads Hall is old but still in use after 50+ years
Off-campus options like Coliseum Lofts reported roaches and cold apartments
key_quotes: The doors are glass?? jfc dude vcu needs to stop accepting so many people this is ridiculous
Sister lived there. Bugs. So many bugs they dont care about. Her and her roomies were SO clean but the roaches still came for them.
I can't believe how much we paid and how much of a luxury we thought it would be. Check out the triples.

key_points: Campus is politically active and often polarized around protests
Club culture described as cliquey, hard to break into as newcomer
Queer and diverse community events are frequent and well-attended
key_quotes: Why are clubs at VCU so cliquey? I feel like all the club meetings I've been to have been super cliquey and weird.
Tons of my friends made the same decision and were SO happy with it. VCU is way lower stress and if you find the right crowd, you'll love it in Richmond!
VCU has always been a well-integrated school.

key_points: Beloved dining worker Miss Peaches passed away, revealing tight community bonds
Campus dining quality disputed — some praise it, others find it mediocre
Students with leftover meal swipes give them away freely, suggesting excess
key_quotes: There's been times when I didn't have swipes or any bread to eat and she'd let me in for the free ski, God rest her soul.
I have never seen any difference in the quality of the food. It is all good stuff.
I cant tell if yall are joking. Ive never seen food look so unappetizing in my life.

key_points: VCU Dental School described as institutionally toxic with student deaths reported
Multiple posts about students needing places to cry on campus daily
Drug spiking incidents at parties reported with some RA response
key_quotes: The amount of people in this program that say it's literal hell is a majority. The teachers are abysmal.
Best places to cry on campus? I am having a rough day already and deserve a solid 5 minute cry break.
This isn't just about mental health, or grief, or support. This is about institutional change.

key_points: VCU frequently chosen over W&M and UVA purely due to scholarships
Non-citizens and DACA students report being cut off from aid with no warning
Middle-class students get little help; costs run ~$18k/semester for some
key_quotes: Money. It was literally just money. VCU will cost me $15,000 per year with the scholarship I got. William and Mary gave me nothing.
I don't know what to do, VASA was my only financial aid help since I can't get FAFSA (not a citizen).
My first year is going to be around 18k per semester according to a financial aid officer.

key_points: VCUarts top-ranked programs (sculpture #1) undermined by AI use and budget cuts
Transfer design students often graduate 1-2 years late due to poor communication
Canvas platform hacked mid-finals affecting thousands; unreliable wifi also reported
key_quotes: If you are a transfer student considering VCUArts for a degree in design, DO NOT COME TO VCU UNLESS YOU'RE WILLING TO SWITCH MAJORS.
They actively spit in the face of the art department so much, especially with the buildings we work in falling apart.
You can't take classes outside your major. VCU Art students are really the only ones who can take art classes. Wish I knew that.

key_points: Admin withheld diploma of student activist for participating in peaceful memorial
Tuition increases justified by stipend raises that never materialized for grad students
VCU stayed open during dangerous ice storms, angering students and staff
key_quotes: VCU is withholding the diploma of a Palestinian American student because of her campus activism.
Michael Rao's 9% salary increase in September 2025 (because he deserves an $895,000 salary) while graduate student stipends flatlined.
Not every school closing except VCU. My husband tried to get to work today and couldn't even get off the street.

key_points: Monroe Park area: flooding, missing bricks, and dangerous ice go unaddressed for years
Grace Street late at night has documented safety incidents including near-assault
Richmond city infrastructure frequently fails students — water crises, flooding, bad roads
key_quotes: How long has this path been missing bricks? It's been here since at least 2016.
I was walking back from studying on Grace St when guys approached me and I heard one say 'let's hit this n****'.
Richmond not having a major water crisis for six months challenge (impossible).

key_points: MLS graduates note pay doesn't match educational demands of the program
VCU premed seen as GPA-friendly with easy research access vs. grade-deflating peers
Arts graduates have achieved notable careers but outcomes vary widely
key_quotes: Its a great profession to get into, my only issue is the pay isnt there for what you're expected to know in school.
From what I've heard, basically guaranteed 3.9+ GPA if you put in work. Easy to get research.
VCU Arts grad, really cool! [on alumni reaching NYC Mayoral home]

key_points: Strong scholarships make VCU a financially smart pick over W&M, UVA, even Duke
Full-ride Provost scholarship exists but requires very strong stats to compete
Cost vs. infrastructure quality gap frustrates students paying premium prices
key_quotes: We declined W&M and UVA 2 years ago in favor of VCU for the same reason. Money.
Would I be crazy for choosing a full ride to VCU + honors for business over Fordham's Gabelli? Fordham would cost me 66k yearly vs like 0k.
While the number of incoming students continue to grow, the resources have not kept up.

key_points: Genuinely diverse, progressive, and artsy — not a typical state school feel
Community is warm and scrappy; students help each other with swipes, surveys, pets
Politically charged campus with real protests, activist culture, and friction with admin
key_quotes: VCU has some of the highest queer, trans, POC, Muslim, Jewish, disabled, neurodivergent, international, and first generation student populations in the state.
I transferred in 2018, VCU provided opportunities that I feel like I would not have received at other universities.
Tons of my friends made the same decision and were SO happy with it. VCU is way lower stress and if you find the right crowd, you'll love it in Richmond!

key_points: **Administration**: withheld diploma of activist student for peaceful protest participation
**Arts Program**: uses AI-generated art in official communications while charging art tuition
**Safety**: multiple reports of late-night assaults, predatory fake casting calls on campus
key_quotes: Disgraceful VCU leadership. [on diploma being withheld from Palestinian-American student]
This is someone who has done predatory modeling scams before around Richmond. He has a pending court case due to luring students into modeling photoshoots then sexually harassing them.
VCU, despite priding themselves in their art program, is using AI slop art in their official email communication. Pretty lame.

key_points: Guaranteed Admissions Medicine program is a real backdoor to med school for strong applicants
VCU basketball March Madness runs generate massive campus energy and school pride
Richmond city itself is a huge asset — food, music, culture, walkable neighborhoods
key_quotes: I grew up a diehard UNC fan. I went to VCU, got my BS, MS, and PhD. I am proud to be a Ram.
A 19pt comeback over UNC is WILD. Let's go, that was magic.
VCU is way lower stress and if you find the right crowd, you'll love it in Richmond!
